steps.title: Identify the rival explanation
steps.content: Martinez and colleagues concluded that H. ochraceiceps associates T. caesius alarm calls with danger. Strong support would rule out a simpler alternative: perhaps the birds freeze or scatter whenever they hear any unusual sound, or whenever researchers approach, rather than because the alarm calls have a specific danger meaning.

steps.title: Compare the controls
steps.content: B concerns other bird species, not whether this species interprets the call as danger. C shows consistent responses to different callers but does not rule out a response to sound generally. D weakens the claim because the birds sometimes reacted before any sound was played. A no-reaction control isolates the alarm call. The answer is A.
